questions.q: In which month and year was the Mountbatten Plan announced?
questions.explanation: The Mountbatten Plan was announced in June 1947.

questions.q: Who announced the Mountbatten Plan?
questions.explanation: Lord Mountbatten, the last Viceroy of India, announced the plan.

questions.q: What position did Lord Mountbatten hold when he announced the plan?
questions.explanation: Mountbatten was the last Viceroy of British India when he put forward the plan.

questions.q: The Mountbatten Plan proposed dividing British India into how many dominions?
questions.explanation: The plan set out the partition of British India into two dominions, India and Pakistan.

questions.q: The Mountbatten Plan led to which act passed by the British Parliament?
questions.explanation: The plan resulted in the Indian Independence Act, which legally enabled the transfer of power.

questions.q: The Mountbatten Plan is best remembered for which decision?
options: The decision to build the Red Fort
The decision to start the railways
questions.explanation: Its central feature was the decision to partition British India, earning it the subtitle 'the decision to divide.'

questions.q: The Mountbatten Plan called for a transfer of power that was described as what?
questions.explanation: The plan set out an accelerated transfer of power, bringing forward the date for independence.

questions.q: Which two nations were created as a result of the Mountbatten Plan?
questions.explanation: The plan provided for the two dominions of India and Pakistan.

questions.q: The Mountbatten Plan was announced in which year of India's freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: It was announced in June 1947, the same year India became independent in August.

questions.q: The Mountbatten Plan is also commonly known by what other name?
questions.explanation: Because it was announced on 3 June 1947, it is widely called the June 3rd Plan or 3 June Plan.

questions.q: On exactly which date was the Mountbatten Plan announced?
questions.explanation: The plan was formally announced on 3 June 1947, giving it the name 'June 3rd Plan.'

questions.q: Under the plan, the provinces of Punjab and Bengal were to do what?
options: Vote on whether to be partitioned
questions.explanation: The plan allowed the legislatures of Punjab and Bengal to decide whether their provinces should be divided.

questions.q: The North-West Frontier Province was to settle its future through what mechanism under the plan?
questions.explanation: A referendum was held in the NWFP to decide whether it would join India or Pakistan.

questions.q: Originally Britain had aimed to leave India by June 1948, but Mountbatten advanced the date to when?
questions.explanation: Mountbatten brought the deadline forward dramatically to August 1947.

questions.q: Which Indian leader gave reluctant consent to partition as embodied in the Mountbatten Plan?
questions.explanation: Congress leaders including Nehru and Patel accepted partition under the plan as the price of independence.

questions.q: Which leader of the Muslim League welcomed the Mountbatten Plan as a path to Pakistan?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ali Jinnah accepted the plan because it secured the creation of Pakistan.

questions.q: Under the Mountbatten Plan, what choice were the princely states given?
options: To accede to India or Pakistan
questions.explanation: Princely states were given the option to join either India or Pakistan as paramountcy lapsed.

questions.q: Which Indian leader strongly opposed partition and was deeply pained by the Mountbatten Plan?
questions.explanation: Gandhi was anguished by partition and had hoped India could remain united.

questions.q: The boundary between India and Pakistan, set in motion by the plan, was drawn by a commission headed by whom?
questions.explanation: The Radcliffe Commission under Sir Cyril Radcliffe demarcated the new borders.

questions.q: The Mountbatten Plan offered the two new states the status of dominions within what association?
questions.explanation: Both India and Pakistan were to become dominions within the British Commonwealth.

questions.q: Which Congress leader, known as the Iron Man, accepted partition as a practical necessity under the plan?
questions.explanation: Sardar Patel came to view partition as unavoidable to secure a workable, stable India.

questions.q: After the plan, Mountbatten continued in India in what role following independence?
questions.explanation: Mountbatten became the first Governor-General of the new dominion of India.

questions.q: The Mountbatten Plan replaced an earlier scheme that proposed transferring power to provinces if no agreement was reached, known as what?
questions.explanation: An earlier draft called 'Plan Balkan' was dropped after Nehru objected, leading to the revised June 3rd Plan.

questions.q: Which senior official, Mountbatten's reforms commissioner, helped redraft the plan into its final form after Nehru's objections?
questions.explanation: V. P. Menon devised the revised dominion-status formula that became the accepted Mountbatten Plan.

questions.q: The Indian Independence Act that flowed from the plan received royal assent in which month of 1947?
questions.explanation: The Indian Independence Act 1947 received royal assent on 18 July 1947, weeks after the plan.

questions.q: Under the plan, the province of Sind decided to join Pakistan through which means?
options: A vote of its legislative assembly
questions.explanation: The Sind Legislative Assembly voted to join Pakistan, as provided under the plan's procedures.

questions.q: In Bengal, a separate proposal to keep the province united and independent was floated but failed; it is associated with which leader?
questions.explanation: Sarat Chandra Bose and others promoted a united, independent Bengal, but the idea collapsed amid the partition process.

questions.q: The referendum in the Sylhet district under the plan resulted in Sylhet joining which entity?
questions.explanation: The Sylhet referendum led to that Assam district joining East Bengal, which became part of Pakistan.

questions.q: Why did Mountbatten famously advance the transfer date to mid-August 1947?
options: He judged the situation too volatile to delay
Britain wanted to celebrate a holiday
questions.explanation: Mountbatten believed communal tension was so dangerous that any delay risked wider chaos, so he accelerated the timetable.

questions.q: Which key consequence of the plan's rushed timeline is most criticised by historians?
options: It took too long to implement
Borders were finalised hastily, worsening communal violence
questions.explanation: The compressed schedule meant the Radcliffe boundaries were rushed, contributing to massive upheaval and violence during partition.
